Paper Title: The SAFE Framework for Evaluating Contextual Sufficiency in Low-Complexity Wearable Movement-Quality Feedback Systems
Paper Summary: This preprint introduces the SAFE framework, a four part checklist that helps coaches and sport scientists decide whether an affordable wearable device that gives athletes movement quality feedback is rigorous enough to rely on. It evaluates how well the system is specified, how accurately it senses meaningful movement, how clear its feedback is to the athlete, and whether it produces a measurable training benefit, offering a structured alternative to assuming any consumer grade sensor is an adequate substitute for laboratory motion capture.
Analytics Title: World Cup 2026 Knockout Stage Predictions: The Opta Supercomputer Forecasts Every Team's Chances
Analytics Summary: This Opta Analyst piece uses a computer simulation that replayed the remainder of the 2026 World Cup tens of thousands of times to estimate how far each team is likely to advance through the knockout rounds. The model continually updates each team's strength rating from results already played in the tournament and converts those ratings into round by round advancement odds and overall title chances for fans and analysts tracking the bracket.
